Container.Name: ServerFoundary
Container.Overview: ServerFoundary — self-hosted web platform for deploying Minecraft (and soon more) servers to Linux hosts with one command. Dashboard + WebSocket agent endpoint + in-container TCP relay for game traffic, all in one container. Requires Postgres (use the official postgres:16 template).
Container.Description: Dark-mode dashboard to deploy, monitor, and control Minecraft Java game servers running on hardware you own. Install the agent on any Ubuntu/ Debian box with one curl command; it auto-enrolls with this dashboard and the platform's TCP relay brings the server online without any port-forwarding on the host. (One-time port-forward on the Unraid router itself is required for 30000-30099, see docs/decisions/0001.)

Config.Name: Relay port range
Config.Description: Public TCP relay for game traffic (ADR 0001). Port-forward this range on your router for players to connect.

Config.Name: Public App URL
Config.Description: External URL of the dashboard as reached by agents/browsers. Used in the one-command install string.

Config.Name: Public Agent WS URL
Config.Description: External URL of the WebSocket server. In production put a reverse proxy in front and use wss://.

Config.Name: Internal API key
Config.Description: Shared secret between the Next.js and ws-server processes. Any long random string; keep identical across restarts.

Config.Name: Relay public hostname
Config.Description: Hostname players dial to reach game servers. Defaults to the host part of Public App URL.

Config.Name: External port range start
Config.Description: First port in the TCP relay range. If you change this, port-forward the same range.

Config.Name: External port range size
Config.Description: How many ports to allocate to the relay pool.
